concepts.title: Direction Ratios and Direction Cosines
concepts.description: Students often get confused between direction ratios and direction cosines. Direction ratios are the ratios of the coefficients of x, y, and z in the equation of a line, while direction cosines are the cosines of the angles which the line makes with the positive directions of the coordinate axes.

concepts.title: Equation of a Plane
concepts.description: The general equation of a plane is given by ax + by + cz + d = 0, where a, b, c, and d are constants. The coefficients a, b, and c represent the direction ratios of the normal to the plane, while d is a constant.

concepts.title: Distance of a Point from a Plane
concepts.description: The distance of a point (x1, y1, z1) from a plane ax + by + cz + d = 0 is given by the formula |ax1 + by1 + cz1 + d| / √(a^2 + b^2 + c^2).

concepts.title: Direction Cosines vs Direction Ratios
concepts.description: Students often confuse direction cosines with direction ratios, which can lead to incorrect solutions. Remember that direction cosines are the ratios of the coefficients of x, y, and z in the equation of a line, while direction ratios are the ratios of the coefficients of x, y, and z in the equation of a line.

concepts.title: Normal Vector to a Plane
concepts.description: To find the normal vector to a plane, remember the phrase 'Any three skew lines lying in the same plane have a common normal vector'. This helps students to recall that the cross product of two vectors lying in the plane gives the normal vector to the plane.

concepts.title: Formula for Distance Between Two Points in 3D Space
concepts.description: The distance between two points (x1, y1, z1) and (x2, y2, z2) in 3D space is given by √((x2 - x1)^2 + (y2 - y1)^2 + (z2 - z1)^2).

concepts.title: Angle Between Two Lines
concepts.description: The angle between two lines with direction ratios a1, b1, c1 and a2, b2, c2 is given by the formula cos(θ) = |(a1a2 + b1b2 + c1c2)| / (√(a1^2 + b1^2 + c1^2) * √(a2^2 + b2^2 + c2^2)).

concepts.title: Equation of a Line Passing Through a Point and Parallel to Another Line
concepts.description: The equation of a line passing through a point (x1, y1, z1) and parallel to a line with direction ratios a, b, c is given by (x - x1)/a = (y - y1)/b = (z - z1)/c.
